claimText: The Equator Initiative Award is presented biennially by the United Nations Development Programme (UNDP).
evidenceExcerpt: Presented biennially under Equator Initiative of United Nations Development Programme (UNDP).
examUse: Statement-based question identifying the awarding body and frequency of the award.
distractorHooks: Presented by UNEP, IUCN, or WWF instead of UNDP

claimText: The Equator Initiative Award is colloquially referred to as the Nobel Prize for Biodiversity Conservation.
evidenceExcerpt: ‘Equator Initiative Award’, also referred to as Nobel Prize for Biodiversity Conservation.
examUse: Fact-based matching question regarding popular titles of international environmental awards.
distractorHooks: Referred to as the Green Nobel (which is the Goldman Environmental Prize)
Referred to as the Nobel Prize for Climate Change

claimText: The Equator Initiative Award specifically recognizes Indigenous Peoples and local communities for their efforts in biodiversity conservation, addressing land degradation, and livelihood improvement.
evidenceExcerpt: The initiative provides opportunities for Indigenous Peoples and local communities to address land degradation, biodiversity conservation and livelihood improvement.
examUse: Statement-based question testing the target beneficiaries and core objectives of the Equator Initiative.
distractorHooks: Recognizes national governments or state agencies
Recognizes multinational corporations for CSR activities

claimText: The Bibi Fatima Women’s self-help group (SHG) from Dharwad district is a winner of the UNDP Equator Initiative Award.
evidenceExcerpt: Bibi Fatima Women’s self-help group (SHG) from a small village in Dharwad district was one of the winners of the ‘Equator Initiative Award’
examUse: Current affairs statement-based question on Indian recipients of international environmental awards.
distractorHooks: Won the UNEP Champions of the Earth award
Is an SHG from Kerala or Rajasthan instead of Karnataka (Dharwad)
